nodes.label: Graph Query First
nodes.rationale: Scoped graph queries are usually smaller and more relevant than broad reports or raw source searches.

nodes.label: Slack OAuth for PM Authentication
nodes.rationale: Slack is limited to PM identity in V1, avoiding an unnecessary notification bot.

nodes.label: Docker Session Sandbox
nodes.rationale: Only the session worktree is mounted, and environment forwarding is explicit, to isolate execution and protect developer secrets.

nodes.label: Safe Agent Metadata Publication
nodes.rationale: The local agent publishes aliases and capabilities while local filesystem paths never leave the developer machine.

nodes.label: Developer and PM Role Separation
nodes.rationale: The PM can drive changes without terminal access while the developer retains repository, machine, and cleanup control.

nodes.label: Validation Gate Before Review
nodes.rationale: Build, tests, lint, and preview must pass before a review request can be created.

nodes.label: External Provider Replaceability
nodes.rationale: External tools are hidden behind ports and adapters so provider changes do not alter core business logic.

nodes.label: Visual Product Reference
nodes.rationale: The prototype is product evidence for behavior and role boundaries, but should not dictate production component structure.

nodes.label: Prototype Is Not Production Code
nodes.rationale: Production should preserve prototype behavior while replacing its inline-style structure with React, Tailwind, and app-owned shadcn/ui primitives.

nodes.label: AuthModule
nodes.community_name: BT-050 — Same-email cross-role accounts remain independent

nodes.label: Role-specific identity
nodes.rationale: Developer and PM identities must remain authorization-distinct even when their normalized email is the same.
nodes.community_name: BT-050 — Same-email cross-role accounts remain independent

nodes.label: Email and kind uniqueness
nodes.rationale: Uniqueness on (email, kind) allows one normalized email to own separate developer and PM users without collapsing roles.
nodes.community_name: BT-050 — Same-email cross-role accounts remain independent

nodes.label: localStorage browser authentication
nodes.rationale: Browser authentication is persisted in localStorage.
nodes.community_name: BT-050 — Same-email cross-role accounts remain independent

nodes.label: Separate browsers or browser profiles
nodes.rationale: Because authentication persists in localStorage, simultaneous same-email developer and PM testing uses separate browsers or browser profiles rather than separate tabs.
nodes.community_name: BT-050 — Same-email cross-role accounts remain independent

nodes.label: Project and session membership
nodes.community_name: BT-050 — Same-email cross-role accounts remain independent

nodes.label: BT-050 — Same-email cross-role accounts remain independent
nodes.rationale: Developers must be able to test both V1 roles with one email without either identity inheriting the other role.

nodes.community_name: BT-050 — Same-email cross-role accounts remain independent

nodes.label: Separate-browser or profile authentication
nodes.rationale: The GitHub developer and Slack PM authenticate in separate browsers or browser profiles so each browser retains its own role-specific localStorage session.
